description.para: These options are used in XTmrCtr_SetOptions() and XTmrCtr_GetOptions()

briefdescription.para: Used to configure the timer counter device.

para.preformatted: XTC_CASCADE_MODE_OPTION Enables the Cascade Mode only valid for TCSRO. XTC_ENABLE_ALL_OPTION Enables all timer counters at once. XTC_DOWN_COUNT_OPTION Configures the timer counter to count down from start value, the default is to count up. XTC_CAPTURE_MODE_OPTION Configures the timer to capture the timer counter value when the external capture line is asserted. The default mode is compare mode. XTC_INT_MODE_OPTION Enables the timer counter interrupt output. XTC_AUTO_RELOAD_OPTION In compare mode, configures the timer counter to reload from the compare value. The default mode causes the timer counter to hold when the compare value is hit. In capture mode, configures the timer counter to not hold the previous capture value if a new event occurs. The default mode cause the timer counter to hold the capture value until recognized. XTC_EXT_COMPARE_OPTION Enables the external compare output signal.

description.para: Register offsets within a timer counter, there are multiple timer counters within a single device

briefdescription.para: Control/Status register.
Load register.
Timer counter register.

description.para: Control Status Register bit masks Used to configure the timer counter device.

briefdescription.para: Cascade Mode.
Enables all timer counters.
Enables the Pulse Width Modulation.
If bit is set, an interrupt has occurred.

detaileddescription.para: If set and '1' is written to this bit position, bit is cleared.

briefdescription.para: Enables only the specific timer.
Enables the interrupt output.
Loads the timer using the load value provided earlier in the Load Register, XTC_TLR_OFFSET.
In compare mode, configures the timer counter to reload from the Load Register.

detaileddescription.para: The default mode causes the timer counter to hold when the compare value is hit. In capture mode, configures the timer counter to not hold the previous capture value if a new event occurs. The default mode cause the timer counter to hold the capture value until recognized.

briefdescription.para: Enables the external input to the timer counter.
Enables the external generate output for the timer.
Configures the timer counter to count down from start value, the default is to count up.
Enables the timer to capture the timer counter value when the external capture line is asserted.

detaileddescription.para: The default mode is compare mode.

briefdescription.para: Signature for the callback function.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked. Its type is unimportant to the driver, so it is a void pointer.

is the number of the timer/counter within the device. The device typically contains at least two timer/counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 to (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: The timer/counter configuration table, sized by the number of instances defined in xparameters.h.

memberdef.name: OptionsTable

briefdescription.para: The timer/counter configuration table, sized by the number of instances defined in xparameters.h.
Default callback for the driver does nothing.

detaileddescription.para: It matches the signature of the XTmrCtr_Handler type.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the callback's data.
is the ID of the timer counter which a user-defined callback would normally operate on.

briefdescription.para: This function populates the timer counter's configuration structure and sets some configurations defaults.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is a pointer to the configuration structure that will be used to copy the settings from.
is the device base address in the virtual memory space. If the address translation is not used, then the physical address is passed.

simplesect.para: Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: (Re-)initializes all timer counters which aren't started already.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if at least one timer counter is stopped.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a specific timer/counter instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: Initialize fields of the XTmrCtr structure, then reset the timer/counter.If a timer is already running then it is not initialized.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the base address of the device

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the device doesn't exist

simplesect.para: In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Starts the specified timer counter of the device such that it starts running.

detaileddescription.para: The timer counter is reset before it is started and the reset value is loaded into the timer counter.

If interrupt mode is specified in the options, it is necessary for the caller to connect the interrupt handler of the timer/counter to the interrupt source, typically an interrupt controller, and enable the interrupt within the interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: Stops the timer counter by disabling it.

detaileddescription.para: It is the callers' responsibility to disconnect the interrupt handler of the timer_counter from the interrupt source, typically an interrupt controller, and disable the interrupt within the interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: Get the current value of the specified timer counter.

detaileddescription.para: The timer counter may be either incrementing or decrementing based upon the current mode of operation.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The current value for the timer counter.

briefdescription.para: Set the reset value for the specified timer counter.

detaileddescription.para: This is the value that is loaded into the timer counter when it is reset. This value is also loaded when the timer counter is started.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
contains the value to be used to reset the timer counter.

briefdescription.para: Returns the timer counter value that was captured the last time the external capture input was asserted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The current capture value for the indicated timer counter.

briefdescription.para: Resets the specified timer counter of the device.

detaileddescription.para: A reset causes the timer counter to set it's value to the reset value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

briefdescription.para: Checks if the specified timer counter of the device has expired.

detaileddescription.para: In capture mode, expired is defined as a capture occurred. In compare mode, expired is defined as the timer counter rolled over/under for up/down counting.
When interrupts are enabled, the expiration causes an interrupt. This function is typically used to poll a timer counter to determine when it has expired.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: TRUE if the timer has expired, and FALSE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Configures timers to generate PWM output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the period of pwm signal in nano seconds.
is the high time of pwm signal in nano seconds.

simplesect.para: the duty cycle that will possibly be achieved.
This function needs to be called before enabling PWM otherwise the output of PWM may be indeterminate. Here Down count mode of timers are used for generating PWM output.

briefdescription.para: Enables the PWM output as per configurations set by XTmrCtr_PwmConfigure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.

briefdescription.para: Disables the PWM output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.

simplesect.para: Call to this function disables only the PWM output and do not alter any configuration. PWM output can again be enabled by calling XTmrCtr_PwmEnable without the need of re-configuration.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The table TmrCtr_ConfigTable contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration that matches the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Enables the specified options for the specified timer counter.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the options without regard to the current options of the driver. To prevent a loss of the current options, the user should call XTmrCtr_GetOptions() prior to this function and modify the retrieved options to pass into this function to prevent loss of the current options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

contains the desired options to be set or cleared. Setting the option to '1' enables the option, clearing the to '0' disables the option. The options are bit masks such that multiple options may be set or cleared. The options are described in xtmrctr.h.

briefdescription.para: Get the options for the specified timer counter.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
TmrCtrNumber
is the timer counter of the device to operate on Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
The currently set options. An option which is set to a '1' is enabled and set to a '0' is disabled. The options are bit masks such that multiple options may be set or cleared. The options are described in xtmrctr.h.

briefdescription.para: Get a copy of the XTmrCtrStats structure, which contains the current statistics for this driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is a pointer to a XTmrCtrStats structure which will get a copy of current statistics.

briefdescription.para: Clear the XTmrCtrStats structure for this driver.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.

briefdescription.para: Runs a self-test on the driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: This test verifies that the specified timer counter of the device can be enabled and increments.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance.
is the timer counter of the device to operate on. Each device may contain multiple timer counters. The timer number is a zero based number with a range of 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the timer is not incrementing.

detaileddescription.para: This is a destructive test using the provided timer. The current settings of the timer are returned to the initialized values and all settings at the time this function is called are overwritten.

briefdescription.para: Sets the timer callback function, which the driver calls when the specified timer times out.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XTmrCtr instance .
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
FuncPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
None.
The handler is called within interrupt context so the function that is called should either be short or pass the more extensive processing off to another task to allow the interrupt to return and normal processing to continue.

briefdescription.para: Interrupt Service Routine (ISR) for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function only performs processing for the device and does not save and restore the interrupt context.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the timer/counter instance for the interrupt.

briefdescription.para: Defines the number of timer counters within a single hardware device.

detaileddescription.para: This number is not currently parameterized in the hardware but may be in the future.

briefdescription.para: Read one of the timer counter registers.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the timer counter device.
contains the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
contains the offset from the 1st register of the timer counter to select the specific register of the timer counter.

simplesect.para: The value read from the register, a 32 bit value.
C-Style signature: u32 XTmrCtr_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TimerNumber, unsigned RegOffset);

briefdescription.para: Write a specified value to a register of a timer counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the timer counter device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
contain the offset from the 1st register of the timer counter to select the specific register of the timer counter.
is the 32 bit value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TimerNumber, unsigned RegOffset, u32 ValueToWrite);

briefdescription.para: Set the Control Status Register of a timer counter to the specified value.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
is the 32 bit value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_SetControlStatusReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber,u32 RegisterValue);

briefdescription.para: Get the Control Status Register of a timer counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The value read from the register, a 32 bit value.
C-Style signature: u32 XTmrCtr_GetControlStatusReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Get the Timer Counter Register of a timer counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The value read from the register, a 32 bit value.
C-Style signature: u32 XTmrCtr_GetTimerCounterReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Set the Load Register of a timer counter to the specified value.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).
is the 32 bit value to be written to the register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_SetLoadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber, u32 RegisterValue);

briefdescription.para: Get the Load Register of a timer counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: The value read from the register, a 32 bit value.
C-Style signature: u32 XTmrCtr_GetLoadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Enable a timer counter such that it starts running.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_Enable(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Disable a timer counter such that it stops running.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_Disable(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Enable the interrupt for a timer counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_EnableIntr(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Disable the interrupt for a timer counter.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_DisableIntr(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Cause the timer counter to load it's Timer Counter Register with the value in the Load Register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XTmrCtr_LoadTimerCounterReg(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Determine if a timer counter event has occurred.

detaileddescription.para: Events are defined to be when a capture has occurred or the counter has roller over.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the specific timer counter within the device, a zero based number, 0 - (XTC_DEVICE_TIMER_COUNT - 1).

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: int XTmrCtr_HasEventOccurred(u32 BaseAddress, u8 TmrCtrNumber);

briefdescription.para: Contains required functions for the XTmrCtr driver.

detaileddescription.para: Contains function to get and clear statistics for the XTmrCtr component.
This file contains static initialization methods for the XTmrCtr driver.
Contains diagnostic/self-test functions for the XTmrCtr component.
Contains configuration options functions for the XTmrCtr component.
This header file contains identifiers and low-level driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains low-level driver functions that can be used to access the device.
Contains interrupt-related functions for the XTmrCtr component.
This file contains data which is shared between files internal to the XTmrCtr component.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of timer/counter devices in the system.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 08/16/01 First release 1.00b jhl 02/21/02 Repartitioned the driver for smaller files 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's. _m is removed from all the macro definitions. 2.05a adk 15/05/13 Fixed the CR:693066 Added the IsStartedTmrCtr0/IsStartedTmrCtr1 members to the XTmrCtr instance structure. The IsStartedTmrCtrX will be assigned XIL_COMPONENT_IS_STARTED in the XTmrCtr_Start function. The IsStartedTmrCtrX will be cleared in the XTmrCtr_Stop function. There will be no Initialization done in the XTmrCtr_Initialize if both the timers have already started and the XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED Status is returned. Removed the logic in the XTmrCtr_Initialize function which was checking the Register Value to know whether a timer has started or not. 4.0 als 09/30/15 Updated initialization API. 4.1 sk 11/10/15 Used UINTPTR instead of u32 for Baseaddress CR# 867425. Changed the prototype of XTmrCtr_CfgInitialize API. 4.5 cjp 03/22/18 Added APIs to support PWM feature. XTmrCtr_PwmConfigure is used to configure PWM to operate for specific period and high time. XTmrCtr_PwmEnable and XTmrCtr_PwmDisable are used to enable/disable the PWM output. 4.8 dp 02/12/21 Fix compilation errors that arise when -Werror=conversion is enabled in compilation flags. 4.15 bdk 12/08/25 Updated comments to support SDT flow for Doxygen documentation. 4.15 ml 02/03/26 Fix codespell warnings

detaileddescription.para: Each timer/counter device should have an entry in this table.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm 08/16/01 First release 1.00b jhl 02/21/02 Repartitioned the driver for smaller files 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style

detaileddescription.para: It is intended for internal use only.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 02/06/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 _m is removed from all the macro definitions.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 02/06/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's. _m is removed from all the macro definitions. 2.03a rvo 11/30/10 Added check to see if interrupt is enabled before further processing for CR 584557. 4.8 dp 02/12/21 Fix compilation errors that arise when -Werror=conversion is enabled in compilation flags. 4.15 ml 02/03/26 Fix codespell warnings

detaileddescription.para: The user should refer to the hardware device specification for more details of the device operation.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 04/24/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's

detaileddescription.para: The user should refer to the hardware device specification for more details of the device operation. High-level driver functions are defined in xtmrctr.h.

para.preformatted: MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 04/24/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's. _m is removed from all the macro definitions. 2.01a ktn 07/12/10 Renamed the macro XTimerCtr_ReadReg as XTmrCtr_ReadReg for naming consistency (CR 559142). 2.04a sdm 07/12/11 Added the CASC mode bit in the TCSRO register for the cascade mode operation. The cascade mode of operation is present in the latest versions of the axi_timer IP. Please check the HW Datasheet to see whether this feature is present in the version of the IP that you are using. 4.4 mus 07/21/17 Updated XTmrCtr_DisableIntr macro to not to clear T0INT flag 4.5 cjp 03/22/18 Added macros for timer/counter instance number and max load value. 4.15 ml 02/03/26 Fix codespell warnings
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 02/06/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's. _m is removed from all the macro definitions. 2.04a sdm 07/12/11 Added support for the cascade mode operation.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 02/06/02 First release 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated to new coding style 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's. _m is removed from all the macro definitions.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
4.0 als 09/30/15 Creation of this file. Moved LookupConfig from xtmrctr.c. 4.4 mn 07/31/17 Resolve Compilation warning 4.13 ml 09/19/24 Fix compilation warning by removing unused variable. 4.15 bdk 12/08/25 Updated comments to support SDT flow for Doxygen documentation.
MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00b jhl 02/06/02 First release. 1.10b mta 03/21/07 Updated for new coding style. 2.00a ktn 10/30/09 Updated to use HAL API's.
